reviews.review_title: This game is really starting to annoy me
reviews.review_description:
Every single time I try to play, it just lags out, so I leave the game, but when I leave the game, I can still hear people talking? And this glitch only happens with this game. Also, im always getting voice warnings when I am speaking normal or not talking. And why is there a 'blob tax' when I sell stuff to people? Like where does the other blobiem go? So im not even getting the amount I sold it for. 

(Edit) Now, my inventory sometimes doesn't even appear, and my game keeps on just freezing. Also, the stupid thing saying "this blob might lag others" is that not what lag protection is for? Also, if it does lag others, they can just hide me. And I think it will be better if you add a feature that asks if i want to hide the whole person or one thing.

Every month i will come up with new ideas, here’s this month’s

1. Add movable legs, this has been a highly requested feature in blobtown, since people have been making more realistic avatars.


2. Add custom animation, this will also be compatible with the moving legs, so you can make your own walk animations or reactions, for example, making a Jax walking animation, Jax has a walk that he does in the show, and a reaction to stuff.


3. Add fingers, this will be cool if you are trying to grab something with a hitbox, so the fingers will look lifelike.


4. Add working remote screens, this will be awesome to have when you are building a home or making a electronics store, when you make it, you will have the option to animate it or just make it one screen.


5. Add lights, what I mean is connecting a light to a switch and having a light, this would be cool in a keyboard, for when you turn the pc and connect the keyboard, you can play games and the keyboard will light up





That’s all for this month’s Blobtown ideas! See you next month!

reviews.review_title: This is the best game in my library.
reviews.review_description:
This game is great for so many reasons. So I don’t think I can list them all here, but I can tell you that the community is great and everybody is friendly. This is definitely my favorite game by far and I recommend downloading it if you haven’t already. 👍

Here’s a small summary of the game:
So the whole point of the game is to create blobs which are just items, and avatars that you wear.

Game modes:
There are also a few game modes. These are blob bash, speed build, and worlds. In blob bash you create your own weapon using the materials that you purchase. (With in game currency) In speed build you compete with other builders to see who can create the best item based off the randomized prompt￼. At the end everybody votes for the best item. There are different versions including team build and quick build. But I won’t elaborate on those since I haven’t played them very much. 

Currency: 
The currency is called blobium, you can use blobium to purchase avatars, materials, blobs, or pretty much anything. You can get blobium from selling blobs or avatars. Or you can purchase blobium with irl cash. But this doesn’t make the game pay to win just so you know. 

CC:
The game also recognizes their content creators! You can apply for CC (content creator) in the discord. There are three levels. CC which gives you the CC badge next to your name tag and some exclusive CC materials. The CC+ which gives you the CC+ badge next to your name and even more exclusive materials. And finally CC++ which gives you the badge next to your name and all the CC materials. The ranks are divided by sub count and number of videos on blobtown. Although I’m not very sure on the requirements. 

Last thoughts:
This is for sure my favorite game. No doubt on that. I definitely didn’t elaborate on everything this game has to offer. I didn’t talk about worlds, maps, or even the physics world (which is basically you get to invent machines and stuff like that easily) But if you want to check that stuff out I recommend you just get the game. It’s free and is most definitely not a waste of space. So I hope I swayed you into downloading this game or at the very least you’ll think about it for the next few days. Buh bye 👋

About a year and a half ago, my brother showed me this cool new game he found called “Blobtown.” At the time, I was looking for many new VR games to play, since I got the device just a few months before. I started playing the game a lot, and then started to learn new tips and tricks, made new friends, and then started learning how to make cool builds. Every new update excited me, and made me even more excited for the next. I started to make YouTube shorts in the game, and I slowly started to gain popularity. The game did the same, and became more popular over time. Blobtown then became my favorite game. I barely visited Yeeps, Gorilla Tag became absolutely buns, and I started to become more known for my Blobtown content. Blobtown became the main game that I played on my channel. I reached 100 subs, and it wouldn’t have happened without Blobtown. I continued to gain popularity, and now, I’m a fairly known CC+ in this game, and public lobbies treat me like a celebrity, which I find hilarious. This game continues to improve every update, and continues to grow in popularity as Rec Room shuts down. I want to thank Mischa for making this beautiful creation and the joy it has brought me so far. And yeah, I know it ain’t that serious, but for real, I love this game, and I hope it doesn’t turn out to be like Roblox 😛😛😛
This game is really good, I love playing it with my friends, the only problem is the community being very hateful and mean. Sometimes you find nice people and other times not so much

I had a really frustrating bug where my shop was completely broken — it said I hit the item limit even though it was empty and I couldn’t remove or sell anything. At first it was really annoying, but after reaching out to support, the devs actually responded and fixed the issue on my account. I appreciate that they took the time to help and resolve it. The game has a lot of potential and it’s good to see the developers are willing to fix problems when they come up.

developer_response.body: Hey there. Sorry for the trouble. First time I'm hearing of this. We are a very small team and we are trying our best to address issues. Can you make a support ticket in our discord so we can look up your user? You can also email us at support@revelvr.com
